materialChanges: Added explicit quantity-dimension and dimensional-consistency-limit claims, mapped them to every scene that teaches or applies the propositions, and expanded learner-visible source notes accordingly.
Extended the NIST locator through Section 7.14 and added JCGM VIM3 as an authoritative, facts-only source for quantity kind, dimension, quantity calculus, and the limits of dimensional matching.
Rebuilt the opening check as a formal attempt-before-feedback diagnostic that tests the declared arithmetic-and-estimation prerequisite and routes learners to recovery.
Added a dedicated quantity-representation assessment item and rubric, removed its spurious mapping from the limits item, and corrected the numeric item's acceptable response to the JSON string "3".
Standardized learner-facing use of the term dimensional consistency and updated accessibility and material-agent provenance disclosures for the final revision.

reviewDispositions.rationale: Added two explicit dimensional-analysis claims; mapped them across orientation, concept, worked example, practice, transfer, and synthesis as applicable; updated every affected source note; extended NIST to Section 7.14; and added JCGM VIM3 entries 1.2, 1.7, 1.21, and 1.22 for the compatibility and necessary-but-not-sufficient boundary.
The final revision preserves the reviewed objective scope and exclusions; the new quantity-representation item improves evidence alignment without broadening the mapped outcome.
The verified 54 km/h to 15 m/s conversion, 375 m distance, reverse check, magnitude check, and constant-speed boundary remain unchanged.
The verified squared conversion remains 0.45 m² times (100 cm/1 m)² equals 4500 cm², with both metre factors and the one-factor error represented correctly.
The verified coating-line calculation remains 1.8 m² and 18000 cm², with time and powered-unit cancellations plus the independent magnitude check intact.

Retained the mathematically correct d = vt versus d = 10vt example and connected its assessed necessary-but-not-sufficient proposition to the new dimensional-consistency claim and JCGM evidence chain.
Retained the NIST source and corrected its locator to include Sections 7.1, 7.2, 7.11, and 7.14 while shortening the rights display string to the supplied schema limit.
Changed item-rate-conversion acceptableResponses from the impossible unit-bearing entry to the schema-compliant JSON string "3", matching its number-mode input and separately declared m/s units.
Added item-read-quantity and rubric-read-quantity to assess the quantity, numerical value, unit, and quotient-unit distinctions, then removed objective-read-quantity from item-explain-limits.
Converted Starting check to a formal check directive and placed numbered answers with reasoning and recovery guidance immediately after the attempt block.

The first three starting-check questions now directly test fraction arithmetic, order-of-magnitude estimation, and division, with an explicit route back to the declared arithmetic-and-estimation prerequisite.

Replaced dimensional agreement terminology with dimensional consistency in the concept, synthesis, practice recovery, claim uncertainty, and assessment rubric while preserving the established glossary definition.
Preserved the sensor-pad misconception repair, including the two-factor area model, explicit squared conversion, and 100-by-100 magnitude explanation.
Preserved the seven-point coating-line transfer item, its integrated model selection, time and area conversion, cancellations, magnitude check, and analytic rubric.

evidenceChecked: All seven scene files retain one level-one heading, logical source order, ordinary reflowable Markdown, descriptive section headings, and no dependence on color, position, motion, sound, hover, dragging, or timing.

Equations, unit cancellations, powered conversions, dimension checks, and feedback have adjacent prose readings or text explanations; the opening now includes a linear nonvisual reading of its cancellation logic.

Formative prompts precede feedback, the mastery assessment is retakeable and untimed, the numeric field requests only the numerical value while declaring units separately, and every task can be completed by keyboard or plain text.

The pack contains no images, diagrams, audio, video, datasets, custom interactive controls, symlinks, remote embeds, raw HTML, JavaScript, unsafe TeX commands, or executable content requiring an alternative route.
Static security and privacy inspection found no scripts, event handlers, data URLs, path traversal, secrets, personal learner or patient data, clinical instructions, or unsafe experimental procedure.

Both references are authoritative public metrology sources with exact locators, factual-reference uses, facts-only original-expression bases, dated access records, terms locations, and concise displayed rights statements; no source expression is quoted or adapted.

lesson.json declares no third-party assets, and ATTRIBUTION.md records the CC BY 4.0 course licence, accountable principal, original synthesis, source boundaries, absence of media and datasets, and material author and finalizer agent provenance.
limitations: No rendered browser, representative screen-reader, 200 percent zoom, keyboard-only, print, MathML, or planned non-web export test was run; this audit is based on the complete static source pack.

Per the operator's explicit constraints, npm validation, strict source preflight, repository verification, and direct inspection of site schemas were not run; targeted read-only checks covered JSON parsing, identities, mappings, answer types, rubric totals, claim/source-note coverage, headings, unsafe-source tokens, minutes, and supplied field limits.
The JCGM/BIPM and NIST rights records document facts-only use and public access as checked on 2026-07-22; this is a conservative project audit, not jurisdiction-specific legal advice.

rationale: The exact original candidate and both advisory request-changes reviews were considered. The two inputs match PREM-QNT-002 version 0.1.0 and candidate commit 4a2d2f874f9ae9cfbc81f97728fdef257f1b007e, cover the required academic and learning-design roles, and declare two distinct run IDs and providers under one disclosed principal. Their non-dismissed COMMENTED transport eligibility is accepted from the operator-supplied context and was not independently queried from GitHub. All fourteen findings are indexed above. The final revision closes the academic evidence gap, repairs assessment and diagnostic alignment, preserves the independently verified calculations and strong instructional elements, and satisfies the final static accessibility-and-rights audit. On that evidence the lesson is suitable for merge after the operator completes the placeholder, runtime-stamping, and repository-validation conditions; this records attested provenance and policy input diversity, not verified human or platform independence.
dissent: The academic reviewer requested changes because the original candidate's central dimensional-analysis propositions lacked a complete claim-and-source chain; the final revision incorporates that objection with explicit claims, scene mappings, source notes, NIST Section 7.14, and JCGM VIM3.

The learning-design reviewer requested changes to numeric grading, objective-level assessment, diagnostic feedback, prerequisite testing, and terminology; the final revision incorporates each objection while preserving the positively reviewed misconception and transfer work.
conditions: The operator must commit this single final content revision with both cited review artifacts, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with that exact 40-character SHA, and make no further teaching-content change after recording it.
The operator must stamp the four RUNTIME-STAMPED adjudicator identity fields from the runtime banner and run the required strict source preflight and repository validation before merge.

Any post-finalization publication transition is limited to lesson status, sourceConfidence, and retained claim reviewStatus fields as allowed by policy; the maintainer, not this adjudication artifact, performs the merge.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev — accountable operator, fresh finalizer and adjudicator
